nodes.title: Guard invalid receiver and callback early

anchor.snippet: if (typeof callbackFn !== 'function') throw new TypeError(String(callbackFn) + ' is not a function');

nodes.why: These checks fail fast with clear errors, so misuse is caught immediately.
nodes.alternative: Skip guards and let errors happen later in the loop.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly more setup code, but much clearer behavior for callers.

nodes.title: Snapshot length and preserve hole positions

anchor.snippet: const len = O.length >>> 0; // snapshot length once

nodes.why: Snapshotting avoids processing newly appended items, and checking own properties keeps sparse-array holes untouched.
nodes.alternative: Iterate with `k < O.length` and write every index.
nodes.tradeoff: Closer to native map behavior, with a bit more logic in the loop.

nodes.title: Invoke callback with thisArg and full signature
nodes.why: This keeps argument order and context aligned with native `map` semantics.
nodes.tradeoff: More compatible behavior, at the cost of a function call with `.call`.
